text:
The third day of theOlympic Gamesin Paris is underway with 18 gold medals up for grabs.

Tom Daley and Noah Williams got the day off to a superb startby securing silver in themen‚Äôs synchronised 10m platform final. The British duo fell just short of gold withChina‚Äôs Lian Junjie and Yang Hao turning in a series of flawless dives to claim gold.

Team GB‚Äôs wait for a gold medal came to an end with the eventing team securing top spot. Laura Collett, Tom McEwan and Ros Canter, sealed victory in Monday‚Äôs final showjumping stage.

Tom Pidcock was another to defend his crown, clinching gold after his incredible comeback culminated in a wild finale in the men‚Äôs mountain bike race and he joinedthe growing list of Team GB medal winners at the Games.

Max Whitlock is seeking to become the first gymnast to win medals on the same apparatus at four successive Olympic Games with Team GB through to the men‚Äôs gymnastics final.

And we will also get ourfirst look at Delicious Orie, the super-heavyweight looking to follow in the footsteps of his hero Anthony Joshua as he begins his quest for boxing gold.

This blog is now closed. For the latest updates, visitMetro‚Äôs Sport page.

Here‚Äôs a look at the gold medal events on Day 4 of the Paris 2024 Olympics‚Ä¶

Artistic gymnastics(women‚Äôs team final),fencing(women‚Äôs epee team),judo(women‚Äôs -63kg, men‚Äôs -81 kg),rugby sevens(women‚Äôs),shooting(mixed team 10m air pistol, men‚Äôs trap),surfing(men‚Äôs and women‚Äôs),swimming(women‚Äôs 100m back, men‚Äôs 800m free, men‚Äôs 4x200m free relay),table tennis(mixed doubles),triathlon(men‚Äôs individual).

So plenty more medal opportunities for Team GB. Until then, goodnight!

Team GB will be delighted with their efforts on day three of the Paris 2024 Olympics.

They added six medals to their tally, moving up to seventh in the medal table.

Team GB medals on Day 3:



A despondent Delicious Orie has reacted to his shock defeat at the Olympics.Find out what he said here.

Much was expected of Delicious Orie over in Paris but the Team GB boxer is out of the men‚Äôs 92kg competition.

The 27-year-old lost to Armenia‚Äôs Davit Chaloyan via a split decision and this one could proveverycontroversial‚Ä¶

It‚Äôs all over for Delicious Orie at#Paris2024√į¬ü¬ė¬©Armenia√Ę¬Ä¬ôs Davit Chaloyan gets the win by split decision.#BBCOlympics#Olympicspic.twitter.com/NUau9Vkqh1

Mona McSharry not only clinched Ireland‚Äôs first medal of the Olympics but also her nation‚Äôs first swimming medal since 1996.

‚ÄėI can‚Äôt really ask for anything more than winning a medal at the Olympics,‚Äô she said.

‚ÄėI‚Äôm not even sure that was something that little me dreaming of going to the Olympics thought was possible.

‚ÄėI am really just trying to keep pushing. That is what I have been training for all year, to be top three, and yeah dreams do come true.‚Äô

Yes! Well done Mona McSharry! Was at her semi-final last night, where she was brilliant, and now look at her!

A rare bit of bad news for Team GB on a largely successful day at the Games.

The Team GB women‚Äôs team are out of the rugby sevens after losing 7-17 to USA, who progress to tomorrow‚Äôs semi-finals.



Mona McSharry has won Ireland‚Äôs first medal of the 2024 Olympic games in Paris with a bronze in the Women‚Äôs 100m breaststroke final.

McSharry finished behind South Africa‚Äôs Tatjana Smith and China‚Äôs Tang Qianting. Team GB‚Äôs Angharad Evans had to settle for fourth.

Swimming expert Karen Pickering said: ‚ÄėTatjana Smith brought that race home, she timed it to perfection.

‚ÄėBut Mona McSharry can‚Äôt quite believe it, the Irish flags in the crowd are waving and the family and friends we saw earlier with the McSharry t-shirts will be absolutely delighted.‚Äô

That moment you realise you‚Äôre an Olympic medallist.What a stunning effort by Mona McSharry to win bronze in the 100m breaststroke.Superb stuff from the Sligo star√į¬ü¬•¬Čpic.twitter.com/V1NBg87CNX

Matt Richards produced a superb performance to clinch silver but should it have been even better for the 21-year-old?

The man himself and several BBC pundits suspect so‚Ä¶

Just 0.02 in it!!Matt Richards takes silver by a fingernail √į¬ü¬•¬ą#Paris2024pic.twitter.com/Ymaef7Npmk

What a performance from Matt Richards. He‚Äôs in contention throughout and finishes just 0.02 seconds behind Romania‚Äôs David Popvic.

That is Team GB‚Äôs sixth medal on what some are describing as ‚ÄėMagic Monday‚Äô and their 10th overall of the Paris 2024 Games.

Over in the pool, Canada‚Äôs 17-year-old sensation Summer McIntosh has absolutely stormed to gold in the women‚Äôs 400m individual medley.

Britain‚Äôs Freya Colbert almost bagged a medal ‚Äď eventually finishing fourth ‚Äď while fellow Team GB star Katie Shanahan was seventh.

√į¬ü¬á¬®√į¬ü¬á¬¶√Ę¬ė¬Ä√Į¬ł¬Ź√į¬ü¬•¬áGOLD FOR MCINTOSH!!!√į¬ü¬•¬á√Ę¬ė¬Ä√Į¬ł¬Ź√į¬ü¬á¬®√į¬ü¬á¬¶Summer McIntosh WINS the 400m IM and has her first Olympic gold to add to the Olympic silver in the 400m free.2 World Championship 400m IM golds, and she‚Äôs now a#Paris2024champion.What a race. What an athlete.pic.twitter.com/Cdv1IWJlRS

The Chinese team looked almost shell-shocked after squandering their gold medal hopes.

You‚Äôve got to feel for Su Weide, whofell twice in a terrible horizontal bars routine to allow Japan to pinch gold.

Team GB came close to another medal but can be proud with their efforts, according to ex-gymnastics star Beth Tweddle.

‚ÄėTeam GB can be very proud of themselves,‚Äô she told BBC One. ‚ÄėIt is 18 routines that we are asking for, which is tough.

‚ÄėIt was not down to one error, they were a couple of marks behind in the end.

‚ÄėIt was always going to be a tough challenge to get a medal and they have still got lots of individual and the women‚Äôs final to come.‚Äô

Japan have won gold in the men‚Äôs team gymnastics final in dramatic fashion, with China falling away late in the competition.

Su Weide twice fell on the high bar in his last event, seeing Team China fall from a position of dominance, with Japan‚Äôs Hashimoto Daiki avoiding the same kind of mistake and winning gold for his country, although it was still incredibly close.

USA impressed and were the unlikely bronze medalists behind China, with Team GB finishing in fourth.

Max Whitlock, Joe Fraser, Jake Jarman, Harry Hepworth and Luke Whitehouse put in a strong showing, but narrowly missed out on medals.

Team GB confirmed on Monday that Charlie Carvell has withdrawn from Paris 2024 due to injury.

The sprinter posted two days ago on hisInstagram: ‚ÄėI‚Äôm devastated, I am being withdrawn from the Paris Olympics due to a minor hamstring issue.

‚ÄėI will be taking a break from the sport and social media for a little while.‚Äô

Japan‚Äôs Horigome Yuto has defended his men‚Äôs street skateboarding Olympic title, winning gold ahead of USA‚Äôs Jagger Eaton taking silver and Nyjah Huston bronze.

Yuto Horigome goes back-to-back! √į¬ü¬á¬Į√į¬ü¬á¬Ķ√į¬ü¬•¬á Gold in Tokyo√į¬ü¬•¬á Gold in ParisDefending his street skateboarding title.#BBCOlympics#Olympics#Paris2024pic.twitter.com/C3ts7U1q01

It‚Äôs two defeats from two for Team GB in women‚Äôs hockey after a 4-0 loss to Australia.

Bottom of Pool B, qualification for the knockouts is not impossible, but already very difficult.

Asked how long he has dreamt of Olympic success, Adam Burgess told the BBC after silver in canoe slalom: ‚ÄėSince I was like 10 years old! It‚Äôs what I was thinking about in the start pool, I just wanted to do that kid proud, that dreamt of this moment. Go out there and give it my all and here we are.‚Äô

Adam Peaty posted a lengthy post following his silver medal on Sunday, which included a statement on his positive Covid test.

‚ÄėI‚Äôll now be focusing on a fast, full recovery to give my best in the team relays later in the week,‚Äô he wrote.

Rafael Nadal snapped at reporters after being asked about his retirement plans following a humbling defeat to Novak Djokovic at the Paris Olympics.

Stefanos Tsitsipas beats Dan Evans 6-1 6-2 in the men‚Äôs singles, with the Brit back in action in the doubles alongside Andy Murray on Tuesday.

title: Team GB add six medals to their tally at Olympics day three | Metro News
description: Latest news and live updates from the 2024 Paris Olympic Games day 3 schedule, with Team GB's Tom Pidcock and Tom Daley picking up medals.
